segments: Primary segment (U.S., Canada, Mexico retail operations)
SRS - Roofing and complementary building products

dependencies: Global sourcing program to obtain products directly from manufacturers around the world
Sourcing offices in Mexico, Canada, China, India, Vietnam, Taiwan, and Europe
Reliance on third-party service providers for payment processing
Dependence on suppliers for product availability and quality
Supply chain network including distribution centers, fulfillment centers, and stores
risks: Disruptions in supply chain from natural disasters, weather events, cybersecurity incidents, international trade disputes, tariffs, customs actions, strikes, labor shortages, shipping capacity constraints, fuel costs, geopolitical conflicts, acts of war, sanctions, public health issues including pandemics
Port congestion and delays, labor disputes, strikes, security issues at ports
Increased demand for online purchases impacting fulfillment operations
Labor shortages at distribution and fulfillment centers
Supplier financial instability and noncompliance with laws
Concentration risk in certain geographic areas for sourcing
Dependence on qualified suppliers meeting quality and responsible sourcing standards
Trade restrictions and tariffs impacting product costs
Inability to sell products due to customs actions including regulatory enforcement inquiries, holds, detentions, and exclusions

regulatory_risks: Changes in federal, state, local or international laws and regulations governing minimum wage, living wage, employee classification, immigration, healthcare, data privacy, cybersecurity, product sale and marketing, transportation and logistics, international trade, supply chain transparency, sourcing of raw materials including timber and minerals, taxes, carbon emissions restrictions, hazardous waste disposal, environmental matters, competition and antitrust, sustainability reporting, unclaimed property, energy costs
EPA compliance requirements for installation services business including lead-safe work practices and potential penalties
Compliance with responsible sourcing standards including prohibitions on child and forced labor, health and safety, environmental laws, compensation, hours of work
Product safety and security concerns including health-related and privacy-related concerns, potential product recalls, litigation, governmental enforcement actions
PFAS-related litigation and enforcement actions involving suppliers
Compliance with payment network rules, data security standards, electronic funds transfer rules
Income tax regulations and changes in tax laws including corporate tax rates
Department of Transportation regulations on vehicles and drivers
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and local anti-bribery law compliance
Consumer protection laws and regulations including pricing, discounting, and advertising practices
Changing regulatory landscape potentially resulting in additional costs or delays
State-specific regulations such as Washington State refrigerant sales restrictions
Data privacy and governance regulations including state privacy laws
Regulations on data-driven technologies such as artificial intelligence
Increased regulatory scrutiny and enforcement actions on cybersecurity, data privacy, human capital management, sustainability, and consumer protection

sanction_risks: Geopolitical or military conflicts or acts of war and related sanctions or other government or private responses
Trade tensions between U.S. and China leading to tariffs on certain product categories
Proposed and enacted tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada, China, and other countries
Retaliatory tariffs and changes in trade policies by foreign countries in response to U.S. trade policy changes
Nation-state actors carrying out cyber-attacks for espionage, monetary gain, disruption, and destruction
Increased cybersecurity threats due to geopolitical conflicts in Europe and the Middle East
Politically-motivated cyber-attacks and supply chain disruptions
